notes: Activation event must be a specific high-value action (e.g. integrating a DB or sending first invoice) — not just login

failure_signal: >72 hours
notes: In B2B SaaS: TTFV >24hrs correlates with sharply higher abandonment. TTFV >72hrs signals critical onboarding friction requiring immediate intervention

healthy_target: Above pre-launch threshold (set from beta cohort)
failure_signal: Flatline or drop after Day-3 spike
notes: A Day-1 spike that flatlines by Day-3 indicates curiosity but failed utility delivery

failure_signal: Continued negative slope from Day-14
notes: Cannot confirm product-market fit until Day-90 cohort renewal rates are analyzed

failure_signal: Continued churn acceleration
notes: True commercial success cannot be confirmed before analyzing 90-day retention and cohort renewal rates

failure_signal: Any single P0 open bug
notes: Hard gate — no exceptions. One team that waived P2 exit criteria to hit Q3 revenue deadline saw 40% support ticket spike and degraded Day-30 retention

failure_signal: Any single P1 open bug
notes: Non-negotiable. Calendar deadlines do not override exit criteria

failure_signal: Exiting before all criteria are met
notes: Duration is determined by reaching exit criteria — not by hitting an arbitrary calendar date

failure_signal: Below pre-defined minimum threshold
notes: Use closed beta cohort data to set the GA activation baseline — not industry averages

failure_signal: Any external user access at Ring 0
notes: Smoke test ring. Advance only after internal validation

failure_signal: Error rate or latency exceeding defined SLA
notes: Monitor API latency and database load. If metrics spike negatively: revert the feature flag immediately

failure_signal: Error rate or latency exceeding defined SLA
notes: Broader load behavior testing. Do not advance to Ring 3 without a clean observation window

failure_signal: Post-launch error rate exceeding SLA
notes: Full SLA monitoring begins at Ring 3

failure_signal: 30-day trial (eliminates urgency)
notes: 30-day trials allow users to explore on Day 1 and forget by Day 5. 14 days forces immediate evaluation and drives pipeline velocity

failure_signal: No advance notice or single email
notes: Send 30-day, 14-day, and 3-day notices explaining exactly when beta discount expires

failure_signal: >1% error rate on simulated upgrades
notes: Hard blocker: verify billing in PRODUCTION not sandbox. One team lost 8 hours of GA day revenue because sandbox billing passed but production webhook keys were misconfigured

failure_signal: <50% in Better tier suggests ICP misalignment
notes: The 'Better' tier should serve the primary ICP and capture the majority of revenue. 'Best' functions as a price anchor

failure_signal: Starting GTM in final 30 days before launch
notes: Rushing GTM into the final 30 days reduces the strategy to a tactical marketing checklist

failure_signal: Starting less than 4 weeks before GA
notes: Minimum 8-12 weeks for T1 (net-new product / market entry) releases to allow beta testing and enablement asset creation

failure_signal: Claiming 100% success rate — enterprise buyers distrust it
notes: Enterprise buyers know LLMs hallucinate. A specific rate with disclosed fallback process builds exponentially more credibility than perfect automation claims

metric_name: AI Feature Usage Cap at Launch
healthy_target: Explicit cap active on GA day
failure_signal: Launching with 'unlimited' generation
notes: One team launched a flat $50/seat tier with no compute cap — a handful of power users destroyed gross margins within 72 hours. Always cap at launch; expand limits as cost-to-serve decreases

failure_signal: >30 days TTFV indicates category mismatch
notes: Reference case: an enterprise analytics product repositioned from 'Business Intelligence Platform' to 'Incident Resolution Analytics' — TTFV dropped from 40 days to 4 days and pipeline velocity doubled

failure_signal: 9+ month sales cycle indicates positioning failure
notes: Same case: 80% deal loss rate in original positioning (competing on feature parity with PowerBI/Tableau); repositioning eliminated the category-leader comparison

failure_signal: >40% spike indicates critical quality or UX failure
notes: Reference: One team that dropped P2 bug exit criteria saw a 40% immediate support ticket spike on GA

failure_signal: 'Total Registered Users' (vanity metric)
notes: Replace 'Total Registered Users' with 'Accounts Reaching TTFV within 48 hours' on the exec scorecard — the former masks churn behind top-of-funnel traffic
